data.description: After watching Anthony Bourdain visit La Puerta Falsa on No Reservations, we couldn’t resist stopping by ourselves for a late afternoon bite.  The restaurant was small but cozy, with only a few full tables of seating on the second floor.  The menu, only posted on the wall as you walk up the stairs, consists of […]

" data-image-caption="" data-large-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-1024x683.jpg" /></noscript></div><div class="cb-entry-header cb-meta clearfix"><h1 class="entry-title cb-entry-title cb-title">Tamales & Ajiaco at La Puerta Falsa</h1><div class="cb-byline"><span class="cb-author"> <a href="https://www.boldtravel.com/author/adam/">Adam & Meghan</a></span><span class="cb-separator"><i class="fa fa-times"></i></span><span class="cb-date"><time datetime="2015-05-05">May 5, 2015</time></span></div><div class="cb-post-meta"><span class="cb-category cb-element"><a href="https://www.boldtravel.com/category/places/colombia/" title="View all posts in Colombia">Colombia</a></span><span class="cb-category cb-element"><a href="https://www.boldtravel.com/category/food/" title="View all posts in Food">Food</a></span><span class="cb-comments cb-element"><a href="https://www.boldtravel.com/tamales-ajiaco-at-la-puerta-falsa/#respond"><span class="dsq-postid" data-dsqidentifier="4449 http://www.boldtravel.com/?p=4449">0 Comments</span></a></span><span class="cb-views cb-element">665 views</span><span class="cb-lks cb-element"><span id="cb-likes-4449" class="cb-like-count cb-button cb-like-button cb-font-title  cb-lkd-0" data-cb-pid="4449"><span class="cb-like-icon"><i class="fa fa-heart-o cb-icon-empty"></i><i class="fa fa-heart cb-icon-full"></i></span> <span class="cb-likes-int">0</span></span></span></div></div></div>
							<section class="cb-entry-content clearfix" itemprop="reviewBody">

								<span class="cb-itemprop" itemprop="reviewBody"><p style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0);">After watching Anthony Bourdain visit La Puerta Falsa on No Reservations, we couldn’t resist stopping by ourselves for a late afternoon bite. </span></p>
<p style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto;">The restaurant was small but cozy, with only a few full tables of seating on the second floor.  The menu, only posted on the wall as you walk up the stairs, consists of a short but well recommended list of plates including the Colombian favorite Chocolate Completo (hot chocolate to dip your cheese and bread into).  We’re planning on making a return trip to try this one.  </span></p>
<h2 style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto;">Tamales</span><img data-lazyloaded="1" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHdpZHRoPSI4MDAiIGhlaWdodD0iNTM0IiB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgODAwIDUzNCI+PHJlY3Qgd2lkdGg9IjEwMCUiIGhlaWdodD0iMTAwJSIgc3R5bGU9ImZpbGw6I2NmZDRkYjtmaWxsLW9wYWNpdHk6IDAuMTsiLz48L3N2Zz4=" decoding="async" data-attachment-id="4459" data-permalink="https://www.boldtravel.com/tamales-ajiaco-at-la-puerta-falsa/tamales-la-puerta-falsa/" data-orig-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa.jpg" data-orig-size="1600,1067" data-comments-opened="1" data-image-meta="{"aperture":"0","credit":"","camera":"","caption":"","created_timestamp":"0","copyright":"Adam Sivakoff","focal_length":"0","iso":"0","shutter_speed":"0","title":"","orientation":"0"}" data-image-title="Tamales &#8211; La Puerta Falsa" data-image-description="<p>Tamales from La Puerta Falsa</p>
" data-image-caption="" data-large-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-1024x683.jpg" class="aligncenter wp-image-4459 size-large" data-src="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-1024x683.jpg.webp" alt="Tamales - Bogota" width="800" height="534" data-srcset="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-1024x683.jpg.webp 1024w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-300x200.jpg.webp 300w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-360x240.jpg.webp 360w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa.jpg.webp 1600w" data-sizes="(max-width: 800px) 100vw, 800px"><noscript><img decoding="async" data-attachment-id="4459" data-permalink="https://www.boldtravel.com/tamales-ajiaco-at-la-puerta-falsa/tamales-la-puerta-falsa/" data-orig-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa.jpg" data-orig-size="1600,1067" data-comments-opened="1" data-image-meta="{"aperture":"0","credit":"","camera":"","caption":"","created_timestamp":"0","copyright":"Adam Sivakoff","focal_length":"0","iso":"0","shutter_speed":"0","title":"","orientation":"0"}" data-image-title="Tamales &#8211; La Puerta Falsa" data-image-description="<p>Tamales from La Puerta Falsa</p>
" data-image-caption="" data-large-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-1024x683.jpg" class="aligncenter wp-image-4459 size-large" src="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-1024x683.jpg.webp" alt="Tamales - Bogota" width="800" height="534" srcset="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-1024x683.jpg.webp 1024w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-300x200.jpg.webp 300w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa-360x240.jpg.webp 360w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Tamales-La-Puerta-Falsa.jpg.webp 1600w" sizes="(max-width: 800px) 100vw, 800px" /></noscript></h2>
<p style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto;">First, we had to try the tamales steamed in banana leaves.  There is only one type of tamale at La Puerta Falsa – chicken leg and pork fat in a creamy rice and corn meal mixture.  There was no heat to it and perhaps a little too much rice/corn to meat ratio but overall the flavors were delicious.</span></p>
<h2 style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto;">Ajiaco</span><img data-lazyloaded="1" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHdpZHRoPSI4MDAiIGhlaWdodD0iNTM0IiB2aWV3Qm94PSIwIDAgODAwIDUzNCI+PHJlY3Qgd2lkdGg9IjEwMCUiIGhlaWdodD0iMTAwJSIgc3R5bGU9ImZpbGw6I2NmZDRkYjtmaWxsLW9wYWNpdHk6IDAuMTsiLz48L3N2Zz4=" decoding="async" data-attachment-id="4458" data-permalink="https://www.boldtravel.com/tamales-ajiaco-at-la-puerta-falsa/ajiaco-bogota/" data-orig-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota.jpg" data-orig-size="1600,1067" data-comments-opened="1" data-image-meta="{"aperture":"0","credit":"","camera":"","caption":"","created_timestamp":"0","copyright":"Adam Sivakoff","focal_length":"0","iso":"0","shutter_speed":"0","title":"","orientation":"0"}" data-image-title="Ajiaco &#8211; Bogota" data-image-description="<p>Traditional Colombian soup with corn, potatoes and pollo. </p>
" data-image-caption="" data-large-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-1024x683.jpg" class="aligncenter wp-image-4458 size-large" data-src="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-1024x683.jpg.webp" alt="Ajiaco - Bogota" width="800" height="534" data-srcset="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-1024x683.jpg.webp 1024w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-300x200.jpg.webp 300w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-360x240.jpg.webp 360w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota.jpg.webp 1600w" data-sizes="(max-width: 800px) 100vw, 800px"><noscript><img decoding="async" data-attachment-id="4458" data-permalink="https://www.boldtravel.com/tamales-ajiaco-at-la-puerta-falsa/ajiaco-bogota/" data-orig-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota.jpg" data-orig-size="1600,1067" data-comments-opened="1" data-image-meta="{"aperture":"0","credit":"","camera":"","caption":"","created_timestamp":"0","copyright":"Adam Sivakoff","focal_length":"0","iso":"0","shutter_speed":"0","title":"","orientation":"0"}" data-image-title="Ajiaco &#8211; Bogota" data-image-description="<p>Traditional Colombian soup with corn, potatoes and pollo. </p>
" data-image-caption="" data-large-file="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-1024x683.jpg" class="aligncenter wp-image-4458 size-large" src="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-1024x683.jpg.webp" alt="Ajiaco - Bogota" width="800" height="534" srcset="https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-1024x683.jpg.webp 1024w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-300x200.jpg.webp 300w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota-360x240.jpg.webp 360w, https://www.boldtravel.com/wp-content/uploads/2015/05/Ajiaco-Bogota.jpg.webp 1600w" sizes="(max-width: 800px) 100vw, 800px" /></noscript></h2>
<p style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto;">Ajiaco is a popular traditional dish in Bogota and the Andean region.  It consists of a creamy chicken soup with several kinds of potatoes and corn and is flavored with the distinctive guasca, an herb grown throughout South America.   We didn’t know it at the time but we were meant to add the capers and cream before eating.  It is also served with rice and avocado.  This dish cost considerably more than the rest of the menu (18,500COP) for reasons we are still unsure of but was on our must-try list before leaving Colombia.  We both thought it was good and liked the new flavor combinations.</span></p>
<h2 style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;">Breakdown</h2>
<p style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0);"><b>Location: </b></span><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0);">La Puerta Flasa, Calle 11 # 6-50, Bogotá, Cundinamarca, Colombia</span></p>
<p style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;"><span style="line-height: normal; -webkit-text-size-adjust: auto; background-color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0);"><b>Cost:</b> 28,500COP ($11.63) for one tamale, one bowl of ajiaco and two bottles of water</span></p>
<p style="border: 0px; margin-right: 0px; margin-bottom: 1.625rem; margin-left: 0px; padding: 0px; vertical-align: baseline;">

metadata.author: Adam & Meghan
metadata.og:description: After watching Anthony Bourdain visit La Puerta Falsa on No Reservations, we couldn’t resist stopping by ourselves for a late afternoon bite.  The restaurant was small but cozy, with only a few full tables of seating on the second floor.  The menu, only posted on the wall as you walk up the stairs, consists of […]
metadata.og:title: Tamales & Ajiaco at La Puerta Falsa - Bold Travel

data.title: Tamales & Ajiaco at La Puerta Falsa
